english: 1. thing, matter, object (= dṅós-po (dngos po) Lex.), rdzas dkar sér-por mtoṅ (rdzas dkar ser por mthong) white objects appear yellow Lt.; rdzas ḱa - sáṅ yód-pa dé-riṅ med (rdzas kha sang yod pa de ring med) the thing of yesterday is to-day no more Mil.; mi-gtsáṅ-bai rdzas (mi gtsang ba'i rdzas) something impure Pth.; natural bodies, substances, from which e.g. medicines are prepared S.g.; materials, requisites, dei rdzas (de'i rdzas) requisites for this purpose; especially for sacrifices, sorceries etc., hence also used as identical with magic agency Wdṅ.; remedy, smyo-byéd-kyi (smyo byed kyi) narcotic, soporific Glr.; ointment, v. rkáṅ-pa (rkang pa) and bábs (babs); rdzás-las ₒbyúṅ-bai bsód-nams (rdzas las 'byung ba'i bsod nams) Tar. 20, 9, not: merits arising 'from works or any material causes', but: the good, the blessing accruing from a right application of rdzas (rdzas), wonder-working medicines, and consisting in long life etc., with which also Trigl. fol. 20, b is in unison, if the Sanskrit word is read dzaiwatrikam; srog-ₒrdzás (srog 'rdzas) provisions, victuals Pth.; in the context rdzas (rdzas) is also found standing alone in the same sense, where it perh. would be more correct to read zas (zas); mé-mdai rdzas (me mda'i rdzas), me-rdzás (me rdzas), also rdzas (rdzas) alone, gun-powder, *dze-kúg* (*dze kug*) C. cartridge-box, *dze-mé* (*dze me*) (a gun) not loaded C.; goods, property, rdzas gaṅ yód-pa-rnams (rdzas gang yod pa rnams) all his property Mil.; nor (daṅ) rdzas (nor dang rdzas) money and money's worth Mil. and elsewh.; treasures, jewels, valuable productions, rgya-gár-gyi (rgya gar gyi) Glr. -- 2. in philosophy: matter Was.; real substance, realities Was.

W. *zi* (*zi*), 1. wind, rdzi-rlúṅ (rdzi rlung) id., also bsér-bui (bser bu'i), rlúṅ-gi rdzi (rlung gi rdzi) Do.; ṗu-rdzi (phu rdzi), or stod-rdzi (stod rdzi) a wind blowing down the valley, luṅ- (lung) or mdo-rdzi (mdo rdzi) blowing up the valley; dri-rdzi ldaṅ (dri rdzi ldang) a fragrant breeze, a wind fraught with the odours of flowers is blowing Stg.; *ṡàr-zi yóṅ-ṅa rag* (*shar zi yong nga rag*) W. I perceive an east-wind is setting in; rdzi-čár (rdzi char) heavy rain with wind, rdzi-čár drág-po (rdzi char drag po) rain-storm Tar. and elsewh.; *zi núm-če or tsór-če* (*zi num che or tsor che*) W. to smell, sniff, snuffle, of dogs. -- 2. in comp. for rdzi-bo (rdzi bo), rdzi-ma (rdzi ma). -- 3. v. zi (zi).

pf. (b)rdzis ((b)rdzis), fut. brdzi (brdzi), imp. (b)rdzi(s) ((b)rdzi(s)), W., *zi-če* (*zi che*), Pur. *dzi-čas* (*dzi chas*) to press, to knead, dough; to tread, to beat (clay, gyaṅ (gyang) q. v.); gál-te tsér-ma brdzis-na (gal te tser ma brdzis na) if I should tread into a thorn Dzl.; to crush, a worm; to oppress, to distress; rdzi-méd (rdzi med) Lex., Sch.: 'powerless', but stóbs-rnams-la rdzi-ba-med-pa (stobs rnams la rdzi ba med pa) Stg. evidently signifies: of invincible strength.

herdsman, shepherd, keeper, frq.; also rdziu (rdzi'u) Dzl.; rdzi-ṗo (rdzi pho) a male, rdzi-mo (rdzi mo) a female keeper; ṗyúgs-rdzi (phyugs rdzi) herdsman, gnág-rdzi (gnag rdzi) neat-herd, gláṅ-rdzi (glang rdzi) cow-keeper; rá-rdzi (ra rdzi) (*rár-zi* (*rar zi*) W.) goat-herd; kyi-rdzi (kyi rdzi) dog-feeder, byá-rdzi (bya rdzi) person attending to the poultry; mi-rdzi (mi rdzi) 'guarder of man, a god' Cs. yet a king might also be thus designated; rdzi-skór (rdzi skor) shepherd's hut = pu-lu (pu lu). Sch. has besides: dpe-rdzi (dpe rdzi) index, register.
(vulgo *zi - ma* (*zi ma*)) eye-lashes (the eye-lashes of Buddha are sometimes compared to those of a cow).
with *taṅ-wa* (*tang wa*) C., to address harshly, to fly at.
pond, gen. rdziṅ - bu (rdzing bu) e. g. for bathing Dzl.; v. also skyil-ba (skyil ba); rdziṅ-po (rdzing po) or -čén (-chen) a large pond Cs.
gru - rdziṅs (gru rdzings) Lt., gen. gziṅs (gzings) ship, ferry.
1. for rdzi-bo (rdzi bo). -- 2. fin of a fish Sch.

pf. (b)rdzus ((b)rdzus), fut. brdzu (brdzu), imp. (b)rdzu(s) ((b)rdzu(s)) to give a deceptive representation, to make a thing appear different from what it is (cf. sprúl-ba (sprul ba)), with termin. case to change into, also to change (one's self), to be changed, srín-por (srin por) to change into a Rakshasa Zam.; to disguise one's self, rnál-byor-par (rnal byor par) as a mendicant friar; rdzús-te skyé-ba (rdzus te skye ba) v. skyé-ba (skye ba); yig-rdzu (yig rdzu) a letter filled with falsehoods, a lying epistle Mil. nt.; ṡá-ru rdzú-bai rgyú-ma (sha ru rdzu ba'i rgyu ma) entrails feigning to be flesh, looking like flesh Mil.; rdzu-ₒṗrúl (rdzu 'phrul) (Ssk. ऋद्धि) delusion, miraculous appearances, transformations, stón-pa (ston pa) to produce such, ₒjíg-pa ('jig pa) to destroy the illusion, e.g. by seeing through it Mil.; rdzu - ₒṗrúl - gyi mťu (rdzu 'phrul gyi mthu), or stobs (stobs) witchcraft, magic; rdzu-ₒṗrul-čan (rdzu 'phrul can) gifted with magic power Thgy. rdzu-ₒṗrul (rdzu 'phrul) is the highest manifestation of the acquired moral <
